content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 8, 2004
D.R. Peck
San Diego Centre City Advisory
Committee
520 6th Avenue, Suite D
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-001

Dear Mr. Peck:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You stated that you are a newly elected resident representative for the San Diego Centre City Advisory Committee ("CCAC"), which is also a project advisory committee ("PAC"). You advise the San Diego Centre City Development Committee ("CCDC"). Your question is whether members of your body must be designated in a conflict of interest code, or rather whether the body is solely advisory.

In May 2003, the Commission adopted regulation 18329.5 to deal specifically with Commission advice procedures for sections 87300 to 87306, pertaining to conflict of interest codes. The purpose of the proposed regulation was to balance the Commission's role of providing advice and assistance with the policy of the Act, that conflict of interest codes be formulated at the most decentralized level possible, and that the role of the code reviewing body is to revise and adopt conflict of interest codes.

Regulation 18329.5(a)(3)(A) provides: "Where the Commission is not the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code in question, and the requesting party is an individual or his or her authorized representative, he or she must first request a determination from the agency and the code reviewing body for such conflict of interest code." Consequently, we decline to advise you concerning your request. You should contact the City of San Diego for further assistance.

_______________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-04-001
Page No. 2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question: Your question is whether members of your body must be designated in a conflict of interest code, or rather whether the body is solely advisory.
sections.conclusion: Consequently, we decline to advise you concerning your request. You should contact the City of San Diego for further assistance.
sections.question_synthetic: Must members of the San Diego Centre City Advisory Committee be designated in a conflict of interest code, or is the body solely advisory in nature?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the Commission is not the code reviewing body for the conflict of interest code in question. The requesting party must first seek a determination from the City of San Diego.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter citing Regulation 18329.5(a)(3)(A); no traditional conclusion section, but clear declination statement provided

embedding.summary: FPPC declines to provide advice on whether members of the San Diego Centre City Advisory Committee must be designated in a conflict of interest code, directing the requester to contact the City of San Diego instead per Regulation 18329.5(a)(3)(A).
